svg.text: Python Interfaces | Virtual Environments | Template Engine | Process Lifecycle | Health Monitoring
Event-driven: triggered per message from Kafka/gRPC
Used for: DNS logs, audit events, SOC insights
Runs as long-lived process consuming stream
Cron-scheduled: triggered on schedule, no stream
No event param — polls external systems
Used for: Vuln scans, periodic enrichment
Spawned per cron tick, exits after completion
if args.cloud_mode and not args.script_schedule → event_handler()
if not args.cloud_mode and not args.script_schedule → event_handler() [gRPC]
_initialize_plugin(): scans user's module for class implementing IEventHandler or IScheduler
→ instantiates the first matching class → plugin object used throughout execution
Template rendered by: pkg/svc/python/template_parser.go Template() function
Virtual Environment Lifecycle (driver.go — per Flow ID)
/src/python/venv/venv-42/ ← One venv per flow_id (shared across data types)
├── bin/python3 ← Isolated interpreter ├── src/my_audit_script_42.py ← User script (from base64)
virtualEnvs map[int]bool tracks created venvs — avoids re-creating for additional data types on same flow
Three Process Types (spawned by Go binary via env -i)
1 process per data type per flow
health checker thread started in 1st alphabetical type
Event flows have health checker in the consumer thread
Manager.runningHandlers map[string]*Handler — keys: "{flow_id}_{data_type}" for events, "{flow_id}" for scheduled
Manager.virtualEnvs map[int]bool — tracks unique venvs per flow_id (one venv shared across all data types of flow)
On UPDATE: all processes for the flow are terminated and recreated with new config. On DELETE: all processes killed, venv removed.
health_checker thread in first alphabetical data type process → checks every 30s → updates metrics for ALL data types in flow
Separate persistent process (--health-monitor true) → checks every 300s → writes {flow_id}_SCHEDULED.json
/health on :8081 (liveness — aggregates all flows), /ready on :8081 (readiness check)
Test event support: periodically publishes a test event to verify end-to-end pipeline works → metrics.test_event.status
